topic: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
researcher_bg: An HCI researcher focusing on AI-assisted data annotation and visual search interfaces. The researcher wants to understand the cognitive load of cross-referencing multi-modal data but lacks formal training in organic synthesis and chemical trade-offs
expert_bg: A Senior Chemistry Data Curator (Dr. Liang) who annotates reaction data for databases like SciFinder or Reaxys. He manages massive volumes of literature and heavily relies on domain intuition to filter out misleading AI suggestions

q_01__shared_root.label: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Expert Annotation Workflow & Cognitive Friction
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Initial Scoping & Orientation
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: The preliminary workflow phase of identifying optimization targets before data extraction begins.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Abstract-Conclusion Orientation
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You always start by skimming the abstract and conclusion to sense what reactions were tested and what the authors claim is optimal before diving into data.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Optimization Metric Ambiguity
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: Authors often don't clearly state what they optimized for, so you have to infer if they prioritized yield, selectivity, or time from messy tables.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Data Fragmentation & Verification
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: The challenge of locating and trusting reaction conditions scattered across different sections of a paper.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Scheme-Table Mismatch
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Schemes are often illustrative or outdated compared to tables; relying on the picture alone misses variables or shows non-optimal conditions.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: The Footnote Trap
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Critical conditions like temperature or catalyst loading are hidden in tiny footnotes or implied through experiment structure, and you miss them if you aren't zooming in.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Methods-Text Discrepancy
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: The methods section or tables might claim one 'optimal' yield, but the discussion text implies a different reality, forcing you to decide which one to curate.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_03.label: Expert Intuition & Gut Checks
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_03.description: You develop a gut feeling about certain data; if something feels off or too perfect, you instinctively double-check before trusting the yield over the AI.

q_01__exp_L2_00_02.label: Defining True Optimality
q_01__exp_L2_00_02.description: Judging whether a reported condition is actually useful or just statistically best in a narrow context.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Yield vs. Selectivity Conflict
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: A condition might give 95% yield but terrible selectivity, making it 'optimal' for mass but useless for synthesis, yet authors often highlight the yield alone.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Suspiciously Perfect Yields
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: When you see a 99% yield or enthusiastic text hype, your instinct screams 'weighed wet' or unpurified data, so you downgrade confidence until proven otherwise.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_02.label: Standard vs Refined Conditions
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_02.description: It's hard to know if a reaction was run under standard conditions versus something more refined, affecting whether you can label it 'optimal' for general use.

q_01__exp_L2_00_03.label: Human-AI Collaboration Friction
q_01__exp_L2_00_03.description: Specific bottlenecks where automated extraction tools fail to match expert curation standards.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_03_00.label: AI Context Blindness
q_01__exp_leaf_00_03_00.description: The AI extracts the bolded yield but misses context like failed attempts, side reactions, or discussion nuances that invalidate the data.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_03_01.label: Override Decision Triggers
q_01__exp_leaf_00_03_01.description: You know instantly when to ignore the AI's suggestion based on visual patterns or layout, but it's hard to write a rule for that visual pattern recognition.

q_01__shared_root.label: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature

q_01__res_L1_00.label: Information Foraging & Navigation Sequences
q_01__res_L1_00.description: Understanding the sequential path and physical navigation strategies experts use to locate scattered data points within a document.

q_01__res_L2_00_00.label: Search Initiation & Pathing
q_01__res_L2_00_00.description: Investigating where the search begins and the non-linear jumps required to gather data.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Identify primary entry points for condition searches
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Determine whether experts consistently start with chemical schemes, experimental tables, or main text to minimize initial search time.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Map frequency of non-linear page jumps
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Quantify how often experts must jump between distant pages (e.g., Scheme on page 2, Table on page 10) during a single extraction task.

q_01__res_L2_00_01.label: Cross-Referencing Mechanics
q_01__res_L2_00_01.description: Analyzing the specific actions taken to verify connections between disparate document elements.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Observe physical comparison actions
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Document whether experts rely on scrolling, splitting screens, or printing to visually compare distant text and tables.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Identify visual cues for data location
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Understand what visual markers (e.g., bold yields, specific keywords) signal experts to stop scanning and start extracting.

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Cognitive Offloading & State Tracking
q_01__res_L1_01.description: Exploring how experts manage working memory and track progress when data is not centrally located.

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Temporary Memory Aids
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: Examining tools or methods used to hold intermediate data while navigating between document sections.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Catalog external note-taking behaviors
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Identify if experts write down conditions temporarily to free cognitive load while searching for matching schemes.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Analyze progress marking strategies
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Determine how experts visually mark a paper as 'in-progress' vs 'complete' to prevent re-work during navigation.

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: Visual Anchoring Strategies
q_01__res_L2_01_01.description: Understanding how experts mentally or visually bind related elements that are spatially separated.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Determine structure-to-table linking methods
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Explore how experts confirm a specific chemical structure in a scheme corresponds to a specific row in an optimization table.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Assess handling of ambiguous visual links
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Investigate workflows when explicit labels connecting schemes to tables are missing or unclear.

q_01__res_L1_02.label: Interface Affordances & Layout Preferences
q_01__res_L1_02.description: Identifying specific tool features and layout configurations that facilitate or hinder the navigation workflow.

q_01__res_L2_02_00.label: Document Interaction Tools
q_01__res_L2_02_00.description: Evaluating the utility of existing PDF reader features during the extraction workflow.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Evaluate utility of reader-specific features
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Assess reliance on zoom, find, or bookmark tools specifically for locating condition data versus general reading.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Compare digital vs. print navigation preferences
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Understand if experts prefer digital searchability or print spatiality for this specific cross-referencing task.

q_01__res_L2_02_01.label: Multi-View Coordination
q_01__res_L2_02_01.description: Exploring desires for linked views that could reduce manual navigation effort.

q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Gauge interest in automatic element linking
q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Determine if experts would trust automated highlighting that connects text mentions to table entries to reduce scrolling.

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
Identify primary entry points for condition searches

conflict.researcher_branch: Identify primary entry points for condition searches

analogy.target_concept: Reading the verdict before the evidence

structural_mapping.inputs: Ambiguous reaction tables and messy metrics
structural_mapping.logic: Require final claim to interpret data value
structural_mapping.outputs: Confident identification of the truly optimal condition

analogy.explanation: The researcher seeks data entry points, but like needing a verdict to understand evidence, the expert requires the conclusion to resolve metric ambiguity before trusting tables.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you open a new paper, do you find you must know the authors' claimed optimal result before you can trust which numbers in the tables actually matter?

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
Identify visual cues for data location

conflict.researcher_branch: Identify visual cues for data location

analogy.target_concept: Reading the review to understand the rating

structural_mapping.inputs: Messy data tables / Star rating and review text
structural_mapping.logic: Infer priority from context / Read text to interpret score
structural_mapping.outputs: Validated optimal condition / Accurate quality judgment

analogy.explanation: Both the researcher's visual search and the expert's ambiguity resolution seek key data, but like 'Reading the review to understand the rating', the expert's familiar concept shows that locating the number (rating) fails without the claim (review) to interpret it.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you're scanning a paper, do you find you need to read their conclusion first to know which bolded numbers actually matter, rather than just spotting the highlights?

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature

conflict.researcher_branch: Catalog external note-taking behaviors

analogy.target_concept: Checking map endpoints before driving

structural_mapping.inputs: Dense research paper / Complex driving route
structural_mapping.logic: Skim start/end to filter scope / Check start/end to plan path
structural_mapping.outputs: Reduced cognitive load / Reduced memory load

analogy.explanation: The researcher expects load management to look like writing notes, but the expert manages load by filtering scope early, just like checking a map's destination prevents you from needing to memorize every turn.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you skim the abstract first, do you find you need to write down fewer conditions than if you started reading the tables immediately?

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature

conflict.researcher_branch: Determine structure-to-table linking methods

analogy.target_concept: Trusting the bill total

structural_mapping.inputs: Complex itemized reaction table vs. Itemized receipt
structural_mapping.logic: Accept summary claim (conclusion) vs. Accept total sum
structural_mapping.outputs: Identified optimal condition vs. Accepted cost

analogy.explanation: The researcher assumes you verify every line item (structure-to-table), but like trusting a bill total, you often accept the author's conclusion without re-checking every detail unless the numbers look suspicious.

assistance_payload.scenario: When the conclusion explicitly names the optimal reaction, do you still verify the structure matches the table row, or do you just take their word for it?

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
Gauge interest in automatic element linking

conflict.researcher_branch: Gauge interest in automatic element linking

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes the necessity of reading abstracts and conclusions first to establish contextual framing before examining specific data points. This ensures they understand the authors' claims about optimal reactions before diving into the details.

pivot.limitation: Focusing solely on this orientation workflow overlooks how interface tools might support or hinder the transition from that high-level understanding to specific data extraction.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher is trying to understand if automated linking between text and tables can reduce physical navigation effort without breaking the expert's cognitive flow.
pivot.compelling_reason: While contextual framing is critical, understanding the efficiency gains from automation helps determine if tools can handle the tedious scrolling once the context is established, balancing understanding with workload.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I agree that establishing that context from the abstract is crucial before diving in, but I'm also curious if having automatic links ready would help you navigate the data faster once you've established that frame, or if it feels distracting.

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
Identify visual cues for data location

conflict.researcher_branch: Identify visual cues for data location

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes that the primary cognitive bottleneck lies in interpreting ambiguous optimization metrics within messy tables rather than simply locating data.

pivot.limitation: Focusing exclusively on interpretation ambiguity overlooks how experts initially navigate the document to find the relevant tables amidst complex literature.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher aims to understand the visual foraging patterns and specific markers that trigger the transition from scanning to extracting.
pivot.compelling_reason: Understanding visual cues is crucial because even if metrics are ambiguous, identifying how experts locate potential data sources informs the design of tools that reduce search friction before interpretation begins.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I understand that inferring the optimization intent is the real challenge, but I also need to understand what visual markers stop your scanning process, because even ambiguous data needs to be found before it can be interpreted.

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
Assess handling of ambiguous visual links

conflict.researcher_branch: Assess handling of ambiguous visual links

analogy.target_concept: Guessing the win condition from a scoreboard

structural_mapping.inputs: Raw numbers displayed without context (Table values)
structural_mapping.logic: Inferring the goal based on patterns rather than labels (Inferring yield vs. time)
structural_mapping.outputs: Understanding what counts as success (Determining optimal condition)

analogy.explanation: Both concepts involve resolving uncertainty to proceed. The target concept shows that like the researcher checking visual links, the expert infers missing info, but the expert infers meaning (metrics) while the researcher infers structure (links).

assistance_payload.scenario: When you find a table with unlabeled numbers, is your main struggle figuring out which diagram it belongs to, or inferring whether they optimized for speed or yield?

conflict.researcher_branch: Gauge interest in automatic element linking

analogy.target_concept: Knowing the win condition

structural_mapping.inputs: Game state (Paper data)
structural_mapping.logic: Identify win condition (Infer metric)
structural_mapping.outputs: Valid move (Extract condition)

analogy.explanation: Your tool links text to tables like connecting a playbook to a scorecard, but like my need to know the win condition first, linking them fails if the text doesn't define what counts as winning.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you find a paper where the authors never say what they optimized, does linking their text mentions to the table help you guess the metric, or is that info just missing?

Identify primary entry points for condition searches

conflict.researcher_branch: Identify primary entry points for condition searches

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes that chemical schemes are often illustrative and unreliable compared to tables for extracting optimal conditions. This mismatch creates significant verification work and risk of error.

pivot.limitation: If the conversation stays on the expert's accuracy concerns, the researcher misses the initial navigation behavior.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher needs to understand where the expert starts searching to optimize workflow efficiency.
pivot.compelling_reason: Combining the expert's verification needs with the researcher's entry point data allows for tools that prevent errors at the source.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I understand that schemes can be misleading, but knowing you start there helps us design tools that warn you about mismatches immediately.

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature

conflict.researcher_branch: Determine structure-to-table linking methods

analogy.target_concept: Trusting the receipt over the menu photo

structural_mapping.inputs: Menu Photo (Scheme) and Itemized Bill (Table)
structural_mapping.logic: Photo is staged/idealized; Bill is factual record
structural_mapping.outputs: Ignore photo, trust bill

analogy.explanation: The researcher asks how to verify the photo matches the bill (linking methods). The expert knows the photo is often staged (mismatch), so they skip verification and trust the bill. This reveals why 'linking' fails when the visual is illustrative, just as text overrides methods claims.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you're extracting conditions, do you try to match the scheme structure to the table row, or do you find the scheme is often just decorative and skip it?

Assess handling of ambiguous visual links

conflict.researcher_branch: Assess handling of ambiguous visual links

analogy.target_concept: Checking a receipt against your grocery bag

structural_mapping.inputs: Two conflicting records of reality (Scheme/Table vs. Receipt/Bag)
structural_mapping.logic: Cross-referencing items to verify consistency and truth
structural_mapping.outputs: Decision on whether to trust the record or the physical evidence

analogy.explanation: Both concepts involve cross-referencing two sources to validate truth. The receipt analogy shows that whether the friction is reading faded ink (researcher's ambiguous links) or finding wrong items (expert's mismatch), the bottleneck is the verification step itself.

assistance_payload.scenario: When the scheme clearly points to a table but the yields don't add up, do you spend more time hunting for the connection or debating which number is actually true?

Identify primary entry points for condition searches

conflict.researcher_branch: Identify primary entry points for condition searches

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes the critical risk of missing hidden conditions in footnotes, which ensures data accuracy and completeness. This concern highlights the necessity of thorough verification during the extraction workflow.

pivot.limitation: If the discussion remains solely on the expert's current concern, it overlooks how the initial search path influences overall workflow efficiency before the deep-dive verification phase begins.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher is trying to understand whether experts consistently start with chemical schemes, experimental tables, or main text to minimize initial search time.
pivot.compelling_reason: Understanding entry points is important for the interview goal because optimizing the start of the workflow complements the expert's focus on accuracy by reducing time spent before verification even occurs.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I completely agree that missing those footnotes is a major risk, but I also need to understand where you start looking first. If we can streamline that initial entry point, it gives you more time to catch those hidden details later.

analogy.target_concept: Missing the asterisk on a price tag

structural_mapping.inputs: A product shelf label / A reaction scheme page

structural_mapping.outputs: Unexpected total cost / Invalid reaction condition

analogy.explanation: Your count tracks walking between rooms, but like missing a price asterisk, the real slowdown happens when you stand in one spot and miss the tiny note that changes everything.

Identify visual cues for data location

conflict.researcher_branch: Identify visual cues for data location

analogy.target_concept: Package picture vs. ingredients list

structural_mapping.inputs: Visual summary (Scheme/Bold Text) vs. Detailed specification (Table/Footnote)
structural_mapping.logic: Assume visual represents reality vs. Verify text for actual components
structural_mapping.outputs: Illustrative understanding (Missing conditions) vs. Accurate replication (Correct conditions)

analogy.explanation: The researcher expects visual cues to guide extraction like a 'package picture' accurately showing contents. The expert's concept reveals the 'picture' is often illustrative, requiring an 'ingredients list' check. The analogy shows that trusting visual prominence leads to missing the hidden critical data.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you scan a new paper, do you ever find that the boldest yield on the page is actually wrong compared to the tiny text at the bottom?

analogy.target_concept: Reading the headline instead of the fine print

structural_mapping.inputs: Prominent visual data vs hidden detailed text
structural_mapping.logic: Assuming main view is complete vs searching for obscured details
structural_mapping.outputs: Incomplete understanding vs accurate extraction

analogy.explanation: The researcher views note-taking as a memory aid, but like checking fine print when headlines mislead, your notes act as verification tools for hidden data, similar to how you cross-check schemes against tables.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you hunt for a specific catalyst temperature, do you write it down because you'll forget it, or because you can't see it alongside the reaction scheme?

structural_mapping.inputs: Reaction paper with tables and schemes
structural_mapping.logic: Scanning standard locations for conditions vs. scanning recipe steps for temp

analogy.target_concept: missing the asterisk

structural_mapping.inputs: Prominent text and hidden qualifiers
structural_mapping.logic: Scanning main content versus checking peripheral details

aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
Identify visual cues for data location

conflict.researcher_branch: Identify visual cues for data location

analogy.target_concept: Conflicting recipe steps

structural_mapping.inputs: Table yield value vs. Discussion text nuance
structural_mapping.logic: Visual claim conflicts with textual reality -> Trust heuristic fails -> Verification workflow initiates
structural_mapping.outputs: Curated data point based on resolved truth

analogy.explanation: The researcher treats visual cues like a clear ingredient list meant to speed up prep, but the expert experiences them like conflicting recipe steps that trigger a gut check—you can't just cook faster, you must stop to resolve the error before proceeding.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you see a bolded yield that looks optimal, do you treat that visual cue as a green light to extract, or does it actually signal you to pause and verify the text first?

aligned_path_expert: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature
aligned_path_researcher: Understanding the annotation workflows, cognitive bottlenecks, and Human-AI collaboration friction when chemistry experts extract "Optimal Condition Reactions" from complex literature

conflict.researcher_branch: Analyze progress marking strategies

analogy.target_concept: Signing a receipt before counting the change

structural_mapping.inputs: Claimed yield in text / Cash handed over
structural_mapping.logic: Trust claim to mark done / Sign receipt to finish transaction
structural_mapping.outputs: Data curated / Transaction closed

analogy.explanation: Both concepts rely on a signal to indicate 'work is done.' The researcher's progress mark is like signing the receipt (confirming presence), while the expert's verification is like counting the change (confirming value). The analogy clarifies why the expert withholds the 'done' signal until the discrepancy is resolved.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you find conflicting yields in a paper, do you mark it as finished reading, or do you keep it open until you know which number is actually right?

Gauge interest in automatic element linking

conflict.researcher_branch: Gauge interest in automatic element linking

analogy.target_concept: Catching a price mismatch

structural_mapping.inputs: Listed price vs. Charged amount
structural_mapping.logic: System assumes match vs. Human checks for difference
